column6: $25K MDF for qualifying Partners in new Agentic AI categories (on top of existing $50K MDF). Variable payments now allowed for professional services. Launch Jan 2026.
column7: 1. Qualify for AWS Partner Network 2. Target Agentic AI category 3. Apply for $25K+$50K MDF 4. Build AI agent solutions 5. List on AWS Marketplace

column6: 0% revenue share on eligible Forge earnings until reaching $1M lifetime revenue (replaces previous 5% incentive). Starts Jan 1 2026.
column7: 1. Build Forge app for Jira/Confluence 2. Launch on Atlassian Marketplace 3. Keep 100% revenue up to $1M 4. Scale to $1M threshold

column6: Temu undercuts even AliExpress for many items via direct factory-to-buyer model. Best for ultra-low-cost product testing and short-term arbitrage. AliExpress better for dropshipping/bulk.
column7: 1. Source trending products on Temu 2. Test with minimal investment 3. Validate demand quickly 4. Scale on AliExpress if proven 5. Cross-list on Etsy/eBay

column6: Successful AI wrappers achieve 60%+ gross margins despite using third-party APIs. Fastest-growing AI companies reach $100M ARR in under 2 years. 28 market signals confirm viability.
column7: 1. Pick vertical-specific use case (healthcare/legal/B2B) 2. Build on OpenAI/Anthropic/Google APIs 3. Focus on urgent daily problems 4. Target B2B not consumers 5. Aim for 60-75% margins

column6: $29-99/month subscriptions. Market size: 900M+ LinkedIn users. Revenue potential proven. Build on ChatGPT API with industry-specific templates.
column7: 1. Build ChatGPT wrapper for LinkedIn 2. Add industry-specific templates (tech/sales/marketing) 3. Offer post scheduling 4. Price $29-99/mo 5. Target creators + B2B pros

column6: Anthropic's Model Context Protocol (MCP) becoming industry standard. OpenAI and Microsoft publicly embraced. Orthogonal solves API key management with pay-as-you-go MCP server access.
column7: 1. Build MCP-compatible tools/servers 2. List on MCP marketplace 3. Solve specific use cases (data access/actions) 4. Integrate with Claude/ChatGPT 5. Monetize via usage

column6: Top Skool communities generate $3.4K-$335K/month. 600 paid communities (60%) avg $376.77 pricing. 170K total communities after $9/mo price drop. Small+Paid+Specialized+Engaged beats Large+Free.
column7: 1. Pick underserved niche (LinkedIn/real estate/culinary) 2. Start with 50-200 members at $5-15/mo 3. Focus engagement over growth 4. Use $99/mo Skool flat rate 5. Scale to $25K+/mo

column6: AI Automation Society is largest AI community on Skool with 236K+ members. AI & Automation (9.4%) and Finance & Investment (5.0%) are top niches. Underserved: LinkedIn professional dev and real estate education.
column7: 1. Research AI Automation Society structure 2. Build competing niche community (vertical-specific AI) 3. Target underserved areas (LinkedIn/real estate) 4. Create paid challenges 5. Use AI for content generation

column6: Hyper-automation combines AI + RPA + ML + workflow orchestration. Generative AI now handles unstructured inputs (emails/PDFs/chats/images) that RPA couldn't. End-to-end automation of admin/operational workflows.
column7: 1. Identify manual process with unstructured inputs 2. Build AI wrapper to structure data 3. Connect to workflow automation 4. Target admin/ops departments 5. Sell as vertical SaaS

column6: Vertical SaaS platforms leverage AI and embedded fintech to automate industry-specific workflows - documentation compliance and payments. Reduce manual labor and increase operational efficiency in niche industries.
column7: 1. Pick vertical (construction/legal/healthcare/logistics) 2. Map manual workflows 3. Build AI-powered automation 4. Add embedded payments 5. Charge per workflow or per seat

column6: ByteDance's Pinterest-style app with influencer storytelling. Interest-based discovery (not following-based) = ideal for organic niche marketing. Early adopter advantage available.
column7: 1. Create Lemon8 account now 2. Post aesthetic niche content (faith/fitness/wellness) 3. Use interest-based hashtags 4. Build authority pre-saturation 5. Monetize via bio links

column6: Social media + e-commerce platform blending short-form content with product discovery. Downloads soaring past 3.7M early 2025. Chinese market expertise transferable.
column7: 1. Join RedNote early 2. Study winning content formats 3. Integrate product links 4. Test shoppable short-form content 5. Build before Western saturation

column6: Hit 40M users late 2025. Decentralized system = more user control over feeds and data. Less algorithm dependency. Early adopters gain micro-creator access at fraction of influencer costs on saturated networks.
column7: 1. Claim Bluesky handle now 2. Build thought leadership early 3. Engage with micro-creators 4. Leverage chronological feed for consistency 5. Position as early platform expert

column6: Numbered 3-5 part series (Episode 1 of 5 or Day 1 of 7). TikTok heavily rewarding return viewers and binge behavior. Focus on transformation/skill/outcome. Dominates Jan 2026.
column7: 1. Plan 3-5 episode series with clear outcome 2. Number each video clearly 3. Create transformation arc 4. Post daily or every other day 5. Drive binge watching

column6: Carousel-first trend with confession-style hooks. Each slide reveals unexpected truth starting with Sorry I lied. Raw honest and bingeable format. Keeps people swiping. Carousels deliver highest reach despite being least used.
column7: 1. Write 5-10 confessions/truths 2. Create carousel slides 3. Start with Sorry I lied hook 4. Make each slide reveal compelling 5. End with CTA or value

column6: 1 in 3 consumers skip Google and start search on TikTok/Instagram/YouTube. For Gen Z: over 50%. Optimize content for social search not just SEO.
column7: 1. Research social search keywords in your niche 2. Optimize video titles/captions for search 3. Add searchable text overlays 4. Create how-to content 5. Track social search traffic

column6: Well-implemented psychological pricing increases average deal sizes by 25-60% and improves conversion rates by 15-35%. Use anchoring decoy effects and value-based models.
column7: 1. Create 3-tier pricing (decoy in middle) 2. Anchor with high-priced enterprise tier 3. Make mid-tier appear best value 4. Add outcome-based upsells 5. Test pricing regularly

column6: 79 companies now offer credit model (up from 35 end of 2024 = 126% YoY growth). Credits give customers predictability and vendors usage component to ensure margins at scale. Managing AI economics.
column7: 1. Design credit-based pricing 2. Base subscription includes X credits 3. Usage beyond = additional credits 4. Predictable for customers variable for you 5. Manage AI compute costs

column6: Gartner forecasts 40% of enterprise SaaS will include outcome-based elements by 2026 (up from 15% two years prior). Intercom Fin AI Agent charges $0.99 per resolution and scaled to 8-figure ARR at 393% annualized rate.
column7: 1. Identify clear outcome metric (resolution/conversion/lead) 2. Price per outcome not per seat 3. Set outcome price profitably 4. Track delivery metrics religiously 5. Scale on proven ROI

column6: Gen Alpha (born 2010+) shopping behavior represents $28B+ in direct spending plus billions more in influenced purchases in 2024. Oldest turn 16 in 2026. Digitally native + purchasing power.
column7: 1. Research Gen Alpha content preferences 2. Build products for 12-16 year old market 3. Focus on digital-first experiences 4. Target parents for influenced purchases 5. TikTok-native marketing

column6: Gen Z spending projected to grow from $2.7T in 2024 to $12.6T globally by 2030. Majority non-white by 2026 (48% non-white). Most racially/ethnically diverse generation. Age range 14-29 in 2026.
column7: 1. Build for diversity-first mindset 2. Align products with social justice values 3. Offer affordable upscale-style brands 4. Target $12.6T spending growth 5. Authentic brand positioning

column5: Rural Gen Z Underserved Digital Market
column6: America's rural young people digitally connected yet systematically underserved. Scrolling platforms designed for lives they don't lead seeking support through systems built for problems they don't have.
column7: 1. Research rural youth pain points 2. Build products for rural context 3. Address unique challenges (access/transportation/community) 4. Authentic rural positioning 5. Avoid urban-centric assumptions

column6: US companies increasingly hiring in Latin America and Asia where salaries are 50-75% lower. Average US software developer: $132K. Latin America average: $31K. Global talent arbitrage accelerating.
column7: 1. Post jobs on LatAm/Asia remote boards 2. Use timezone-compatible regions 3. Pay 1.5-2x local rate (still 50%+ savings) 4. Build distributed team infrastructure 5. Focus on senior production-ready talent

column6: US will reach 1.2M+ software engineer shortage by 2026. Cybersecurity gap: 700K US 4M global. 90%+ of global orgs will face IT-skill shortages by 2026. Most painful: senior production-ready engineers in AI/cloud/security.
column7: 1. Upskill existing team in AI/cloud/security 2. Hire globally for senior talent 3. Build training programs internally 4. Partner with bootcamps for pipeline 5. Offer remote-first to access global talent

column5: Web Scraping API 30-40% Faster with AI
column6: AI-powered scraping delivers 30-40% faster data extraction and achieves 99.5% accuracy on dynamic JavaScript-heavy websites. Market leaders: Zyte Bright Data Oxylabs.
column7: 1. Build niche data product (e-commerce/social/SERP) 2. Use Zyte/Bright Data API infrastructure 3. Add AI for extraction accuracy 4. Package as industry-specific datasets 5. Sell via API or data feeds

column6: Bright Data leads in dataset scale and coverage (Amazon/Walmart/Target/Shein). Use cases: rank tracking affiliate marketing market research share of voice. Social media data powers market research to competitive intelligence to AI training.
column7: 1. Scrape e-commerce platforms (Amazon/Walmart/Shein) 2. Structure data (pricing/reviews/rankings) 3. Build API access or data feeds 4. Target use cases (affiliate/research/AI training) 5. Subscription pricing model
